extracted_fields.conditionComments: Typical for age, no major deficiencies noted

failed_rules.message: Delayed Financing is not an allowed loan purpose for 2nd Lien - FAIL
US Citizens are not eligible for Select ITIN program.
Loan amount $406,250 below minimum requirement $3,500,001
Occupancy 'Investment Property' not eligible. Available: Primary Residence
Super Jumbo program only available for Primary (Owner Occupied) occupancy - Non-Owner Occupied not eligible
US Citizens are not eligible for Foreign National program - FAIL
DSCR Multi (5-8 Units): Only Residential 5-8 Units and Mixed Use 2-8 Units are eligible
Delayed Financing is not an allowed loan purpose for Investor DSCR No Ratio - FAIL

income: DSCR RATIOS AND RENTAL INCOME REQUIREMENTS

specifications: Must be current and valid government-issued photo identification
Must match borrower name exactly as shown on loan application
Must include clear photo and signature
Acceptable forms: driver's license, passport, state ID, military ID

reasons_needed.reason: Required for borrower identity verification and compliance with lending regulations
Ensures consistency of borrower identity across all loan documentation

specifications: Must be fully executed and signed by borrower
Must authorize release of tax transcript information
Must cover tax years relevant to income verification period
Must be dated and include borrower's current address

reasons_needed.reason: Required per FULL DOCUMENTATION guidelines for income verification
Enables third-party verification of tax return information

specifications: Must clearly identify property as investment property
Must confirm borrower will not occupy property as primary residence
Must be signed and dated by borrower
Must align with investment property LTV limits (75% maximum per guidelines)

reasons_needed.reason: Investment property occupancy must be verified per OCCUPANCY guidelines
Occupancy type determines LTV limits, reserve requirements, and documentation standards

document_requests.property_address: 425 LAUREL ST UNIT 23, SAN CARLOS, CA 94070

specifications: Must be completed by licensed appraiser
Must identify subject property address: 1234 Elm Street, Los Angeles, CA 90210
Must show market rent opinion for investment property
Must be used for DSCR calculation purposes
Must reflect current market conditions
Must include comparable rental properties analysis
reasons_needed: DSCR loan requires rental income verification via Form 1007
Market rent opinion needed for DSCR ratio calculation
Required for investment property income qualification on DSCR loans

specifications: Must equal at least 6 months of PITIA
Must cover subject property: 1234 Elm Street, Los Angeles, CA 90210
Must be in effect at closing
Blanket policies covering subject property are acceptable
Must name borrower as insured party
reasons_needed: Required for all DSCR loans per NQMF guidelines
Protects rental income stream for investment property
Mandatory risk mitigation for DSCR program

specifications: Must cover most recent 2 months
Must include all pages of statements
Must show borrower name (Aubrey Banaga)
Must show financial institution name
Must show account number or masked account identifier
Must show beginning and ending balances
Must show transaction history
Must be dated within 90 days of note date
Must support verification of funds to close
Must identify any large deposits for potential sourcing
reasons_needed: Assets must be verified for funds to close per NQMF guidelines
Asset documentation required within 90 days of note date
Account ownership must be confirmed for borrower
Transaction history needed to identify any large deposits requiring sourcing
Verification of sufficient liquid assets for closing costs and debt payoff
document_requests.guideline_reference: ASSETS section - loan files must evidence sufficient funds from acceptable sources

specifications: Must calculate required reserve months for investment property
Must show PITIA calculation basis
Must identify acceptable asset sources for reserves
Must show borrower has sufficient verified assets to meet reserve requirement
Must account for any overlay reserve requirements if applicable
reasons_needed: Investment properties typically require reserves
Reserve calculation must be supported by verified assets
Ensures borrower has sufficient post-closing liquidity
Required to demonstrate ongoing ability to service debt
document_requests.guideline_reference: ASSETS section - applicable reserves must be evidenced

specifications: Must be tri-merge or Residential Mortgage Credit Report (RMCR)
Must provide merged credit information from all 3 major national credit repositories (Experian, TransUnion, Equifax)
Must include all borrowers on the loan
Must show credit scores from all bureaus
Must include verification of all credit references provided on loan application
Must certify results of public record searches for each city where borrower has resided in last 2 years
Must show tradeline details and payment history
Must show mortgage history if available on credit report
Must show inquiries, public records, disputes, collections, charge-offs, and alerts
Must be dated within allowed recency window per program guidelines
reasons_needed: Credit score verification required to validate DSCR program eligibility
NQMF guidelines require tri-merge credit report for all borrowers
Credit report needed to verify tradeline requirements are met
Required to identify any credit events, inquiries, or disputes
Mortgage history verification needed for investment property refinance
Public record search certification required per NQMF guidelines
document_requests.additional_notes: DSCR loan with FICO 800 - expect standard credit report will show sufficient tradeline history and mortgage payment history

specifications: Must identify subject property address at 1234 Cedar Road, El Sobrante, CA 94803
Must identify property type (single-family residence) and units (1 unit)
Must show appraised value to support LTV/CLTV calculation for $406,250 loan amount
Must be dated within 365 days of the Note date with recertification if exceeding 120 days
Must include all pages and addenda
Must include required form type (Form 1004 for single-family residence)
Must include Form 1007 Rent Schedule as this is an investment property using rental income for DSCR qualification
Must identify borrower/owner information and reconcile with loan file
Must disclose repairs, adverse conditions, or inspection requirements
Must include comparable sales and appraiser certification
Must include exterior building sketch with dimensions and floor plan
Must include original color photographs of front, street, and rear views
Must include interior photos of kitchen, all bathrooms, main living area, and any areas with physical deterioration
reasons_needed: Collateral valuation is required for underwriting all loan transactions
LTV/CLTV eligibility depends on appraised value for the $406,250 loan amount
Property type and unit count affect program eligibility
Form 1007 is required for investment properties using rental income in DSCR calculation
Subject property identity must be consistent across the loan file
NQMF guidelines require completed appraisal report on all loan transactions to assess adequacy of property as collateral

document_requests.document_name: Fannie Mae Selling System Review (SSR) / UCDP Findings
specifications: Must identify subject property at 1234 Cedar Road, El Sobrante, CA 94803
Must provide SSR score of 2.5 or less for appraisal review acceptance
Must address any data integrity flags or quality concerns
Must be generated from submitted appraisal report
reasons_needed: NQMF Appraisal Review Process requires secondary appraisal product to support appraisal value
SSR score of 2.5 or less is acceptable review product under guidelines
Required for collateral validation and risk assessment

specifications: Must identify subject property at 1234 Cedar Road, El Sobrante, CA 94803
Must be provided by Federal Emergency Management Agency (FEMA)
Must accurately reflect flood zone designation
Must indicate whether property is in Special Flood Hazard Area (SFHA)
Must show flood zone designation (A, V, or other)
Must align with appraisal report flood zone information
reasons_needed: NQMF guidelines require determination of flood zone status for all properties
Required to determine if flood insurance is mandatory
Properties in Flood Zone A or V require flood insurance and community participation in FEMA program
Flood insurance requirement determination affects closing requirements

specifications: Must name all persons holding title to subject property as named insured
Must show property address as 1234 Cedar Road, El Sobrante, CA 94803
Must include standard or union mortgagee clause (without contribution)
Must provide replacement cost coverage (not actual cash value)
Must cover required perils: fire, lightning, explosion, windstorm, hail, smoke, aircraft, vehicles, riot or civil commotion
Must be effective for at least 60 days after funding date
Must include policy number, coverage amounts, deductible, and premium information
Must require written notice to mortgagee before cancellation
reasons_needed: Hazard insurance is required on all properties to protect against loss or damage
NQMF guidelines require standard extended coverage endorsement with specified perils
Mortgagee clause protects lender's interest in the collateral
Replacement cost basis required - actual cash value policies not acceptable

specifications: Must cover subject property at 1234 Cedar Road, El Sobrante, CA 94803
Must provide coverage equal to at least 6 months of PITIA (Principal, Interest, Taxes, Insurance, Association fees)
Must name borrower as insured party
Must include policy effective dates, coverage amount, and premium information
Blanket policies covering the subject property are acceptable
reasons_needed: NQMF guidelines specifically require rent loss insurance for DSCR loans
Required coverage of 6 months PITIA protects against rental income loss
Investment property rental income risk mitigation for DSCR qualification

specifications: Must identify borrower Aubrey Banaga as owner/vested party
Must identify subject property address and legal description for CA investment property
Must disclose liens, judgments, easements, exceptions, and title requirements
Must show title insurer and effective date
Must be dated within acceptable recency window
Must reconcile vesting with borrower and refinance transaction structure
reasons_needed: Title must confirm ownership, lien position, and insurable interest for refinance transaction
Vesting and subject property must align with loan file per VESTING guidelines
Liens and exceptions may affect closing conditions and underwriting approval
Required for underwriter to verify clear title before loan approval

specifications: Must show current ownership by borrower Aubrey Banaga
Must identify subject property with legal description
Must show how title is held (individual, joint tenancy, etc.)
Must be recorded deed establishing current vesting
Must align with Title Commitment vesting information
reasons_needed: Confirms property ownership and current vesting per VESTING guidelines
Required to verify borrower has legal right to refinance the property
Final title policy vesting should reflect the name(s) of the individual borrower(s)
Essential for underwriter to confirm borrower ownership before loan approval

specifications: Must identify creditor/servicer of existing mortgage
Must identify account number for current loan being refinanced
Must show exact payoff amount with good-through date
Must identify subject property as collateral
Must include per diem interest charges if applicable
Must be current and dated within acceptable timeframe
reasons_needed: Existing liens must be paid at closing for refinance transaction
Payoff amount is required to calculate funds to close and verify loan purpose
Essential for title clearance and establishing new first lien position
Required for underwriter to verify refinance transaction structure

document_requests.document_type: Borrower Certification as to Business Purpose
specifications: Must identify borrower Aubrey Banaga
Must identify subject property or transaction
Must state business purpose as required for DSCR loan
Must be signed and dated by borrower
Must be consistent with investment property occupancy and transaction purpose
Must not conflict with non-owner-occupied representations
Business Purpose & Occupancy Affidavit form required per NQMF guidelines
reasons_needed: Business purpose must be documented for DSCR investment property transactions per NQMF guidelines
Investment properties and all DSCR loans must be Business Purpose per guidelines
Required on all Business Purpose loans to declare property is for commercial business or investment purpose only
Occupancy and business purpose representations must be consistent for underwriting approval
Non-TRID transaction compliance requires proper business purpose documentation

STEP_00.summary: Parsed loan file for Aubrey Banaga's $406,250 investment property refinance in CA with LTV 65% and FICO 800. Identified as DSCR loan with 8 submitted documents including appraisal, URLA 1003, Form 1007, and borrower authorization. No contradictions detected. Documents routed to crosscutting (5), property_appraisal (2), and compliance (1) facets.

STEP_01.summary: Generated core cross-cutting document requests for Aubrey Banaga's investment property refinance. Identified three mandatory documents: Government-Issued Photo ID for borrower identity verification, IRS Form 4506-C for tax transcript authorization as required by FULL DOCUMENTATION guidelines, and Occupancy Certification to verify investment property status per OCCUPANCY guidelines. No overlays provided so no overlay conflicts detected.

STEP_02.summary: Analyzed income requirements for DSCR loan and generated two mandatory income documents: Form 1007 Rent Schedule for market rent opinion required in DSCR calculation, and Rent Loss Insurance covering 6 months PITIA as required by NQMF guidelines. No other income documentation needed since DSCR loans qualify based on property cash flow rather than borrower personal income.

No gift funds, large deposits, or business funds indicated in this refinance scenario.

STEP_06.summary: Generated title and closing document requests for Aubrey Banaga's investment property refinance. Applied strict relevance gates focusing only on documents needed for underwriting approval. Identified three mandatory documents: Title Commitment to verify ownership, lien position, and title exceptions; Vesting Deed to confirm borrower's legal ownership rights; and Payoff Statement for existing mortgage to establish refinance structure and calculate funds to close. Did not request closing-stage documents like Closing Disclosure or Settlement Statement as these are generated at closing, not needed for underwriting review.

STEP_07.summary: Generated compliance document requests for Aubrey Banaga's DSCR investment property refinance. Applied strict relevance gates and identified one mandatory document: Business Purpose Affidavit required by NQMF guidelines for all DSCR loans and investment properties. The affidavit declares the property is for commercial business or investment purpose only and ensures consistency with non-owner-occupied status. Did not request duplicate documents already handled by cross-cutting module (ID, authorization, occupancy certification) or predictive underwriting items not triggered by specific loan facts. No ITIN, entity, trust, or citizenship documentation needed for this individual borrower scenario.

STEP_08.summary: Completed final document request merger and ranking for Aubrey Banaga's investment property refinance. Merged 17 individual document requests from all modules into 14 unique documents by consolidating duplicate requests based on document type and context. Applied priority ranking with 5 P1 critical documents and 9 P2 standard documents. All 14 documents have "needed" status as none exist in current inventory. Final output includes comprehensive specifications, consolidated reasons, and proper traceability to guidelines and overlays.
